clinical_criteria: Male patients of 35 years of age or more
ICD-9-CM / ICD-10-CM diagnosis of prostate cancer
At least two encounters before first treatment
At least one clinical notes before first treatment
At least one surgical procedure before the first treatment
Radiology report descriptions should include: NUC BONE SCAN, NM BONE WHOLE BODY, NM BONE SCAN
algorithm_summary: This phenotype algorithm identifies bone scan utilization in prostate cancer patients by analyzing structured data (CPT/ICD procedure codes) and unstructured text (radiology reports and clinical notes). The cohort includes male patients 35+ years with prostate cancer diagnosis who have adequate encounter history before treatment. Cases are defined by documentation of bone scans (CPT codes 78300-78315, 8320) or specific text phrases in radiology reports. Controls are patients without bone scan documentation before treatment. The algorithm identifies various treatment modalities (prostatectomy, radiation, chemotherapy, hormonal therapy) using specific CPT and ICD codes to establish treatment timelines.
